transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: First National Bank, good morning. This is Dani. Can I take your full name and date of birth for security?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Hi. It's Alice Greenberg, fourteenth of June 1991.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Alice, you're all verified. How can I help today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I transferred five hundred pounds to my sister three days ago and she still hasn't received it. She's with Lloyds. I'm worried something went wrong.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I understand how worrying that must be. Let me look at the transfer. I can see the payment left your account on Tuesday via Faster Payments. Do you have the sort code and account number you sent it to?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Those match what we have. The transfer shows as successfully sent on our end, which means the issue is with the receiving bank's processing. I'd recommend your sister also contact Lloyds and quote the payment reference PAY-2024-7765. I'm also raising a payment trace request now.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Should I do both at the same time?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes, it's best to run both simultaneously. Trace reference is TRC-2024-4411 and you'll receive an email update within twenty-four to forty-eight hours. Is there anything else?
